# Aspirational byte-identical fixture set
#
# Each line names a fixture whose `skotch.norm.txt` MUST match its
# `kotlinc.norm.txt` byte-for-byte (after normalization in
# skotch-classfile-norm). The list is intentionally small so each
# entry is a deliberate, defended guarantee rather than a side-effect
# of a generated list — these are the high-leverage Kotlin idioms
# where any regression is a red flag.
#
# Format:
#   <fixture-name> # <reason>
#
# Maintenance:
#   - To remove an entry, justify in the PR commit message why exact
#     parity is no longer required for that idiom.
#   - To add an entry, the fixture must already be byte-identical in
#     the regular parity report; this list is for *locking in*, not
#     proposing aspirations that don't yet hold.
#
# Coverage rationale: pick one representative fixture per Kotlin
# language area, choosing the simplest one that exercises the idiom.

# ── Basics ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
01-fun-main-empty            # empty `fun main()` — establishes the wrapper-class shape
02-println-string-literal    # `println("…")` — string CP entry + System.out + PrintStream.println
03-println-int-literal       # `println(42)` — ICONST/BIPUSH + integer overload selection
04-val-string                # `val s = "…"` — top-level val inlining + LDC pattern
05-string-template-simple    # `"$name"` — StringTemplate lowering shape

# ── Control flow ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
07-if-expression             # `if (c) a else b` as expression — branch lowering
121-while-fibonacci          # `while (cond) { … }` — back-edge + StackMapTable basics
129-for-loop-sum             # `for (i in 0..n) { … }` — range iteration desugaring
127-when-multi-branch        # `when` with multiple branches — tableswitch/lookupswitch
130-when-no-subject          # `when { x > 0 -> … }` — subject-less when as if-chain

# ── Classes & objects ───────────────────────────────────────────────
20-class-empty               # `class Empty` — ctor + default Object super
21-data-class                # `data class P(val x, val y)` — equals/hashCode/toString shape
27-companion-object          # `companion object { fun zero() = … }` — Companion field + dispatch
28-object-decl               # `object Singleton` — INSTANCE static field + clinit
33-interface-impl            # `interface Greet; class C : Greet` — invokeinterface vs invokevirtual
325-object-methods           # object with multiple methods — method ordering on companion

# ── Smart casts & nullables ─────────────────────────────────────────
31-null-safety-elvis         # `x ?: default` — elvis operator desugaring
32-smart-cast                # `if (x is String) { x.length }` — local smart-cast slot reuse
350-null-smart-cast          # `if (x != null) { x.foo() }` — null-check smart cast

# ── Functions ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
97-operator-plus             # `operator fun plus(other: T)` — operator overload lowering
99-operator-invoke           # `operator fun invoke(...)` — call-site dispatch
142-extension-int            # `fun Int.foo()` — extension fn lowering (static + receiver as arg)
292-default-params           # `fun f(x: Int = 0)` — `$default` synthetic + mask

# ── Try/catch ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
267-try-finally-with-code    # `try { … } finally { … }` — exception table + finally inlining

# ── Sealed class ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
26-sealed-class              # `sealed class Shape` — sealed hierarchy basics

# ── @JvmStatic / companion delegate ─────────────────────────────────
1234-jvmstatic-companion     # `@JvmStatic fun describe()` — synthesized static delegate on outer class

# ── Data class extras ───────────────────────────────────────────────
308-data-class-tostring      # `data class … { toString() }` — concat-based toString shape

# ── Suspend ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
391-suspend-state-machine    # single-suspension-point coroutine — tableswitch dispatcher shape
1000-suspend-tail-call-string # suspend tail call — return-from-suspend pattern
